steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence has two independent clauses joined by 'and.' The second clause has the plural subject 'its theoretical linguistic properties' and needs a finite main verb at the blank.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'having reflected': A perfect participle — non-finite, so it cannot serve as the main verb of an independent clause. This leaves the second clause without a verb. ✗
  • Choice B — 'reflect': A finite plural verb agreeing with the plural subject 'properties.' Completes the independent clause correctly. ✓
  • Choice C — 'reflecting': A present participle — non-finite, same problem as A. No main verb for the clause. ✗
  • Choice D — 'that reflect': Introduces a relative clause modifying 'properties,' which leaves the independent clause without its own main verb. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
